title: Majana galeata

The Majana galeata is native to tropical Africa, and its classification is closely related to other plants in the Rubiaceae family. Majana galeata has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions due to its anti-inflammatory properties. However, the Mexican Prickly Poppy (Argemone mexicana) has the following properties:

 The Mexican Prickly Poppy is a plant species in the poppy family (Papaveraceae) and is native to the Americas. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties.

 The health benefits of the Mexican Prickly Poppy include treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, skin conditions, and respiratory issues due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

 The bioactive constituents of the Mexican Prickly Poppy include alkaloids (sanguinarine and dihydrosanguinarine), flavonoids, and saponins.

 The most common herbal preparations made from the Mexican Prickly Poppy include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
